campaigns.name: First Visit
campaigns.description: Fires once — the very first time a user opens this page.

steps.title: first_visit
steps.intro:
This tour appeared because this is your first visit.

Trigger: first_visit
Frequency: once — you will not see this again.

campaigns.name: Page Load
campaigns.description: Fires 5 seconds after the page finishes loading.

steps.title: page_load
steps.intro:
Trigger: page_load
Fires after the page finishes loading, with a 5 second delay.

Frequency: session — resets on each new browser session.

campaigns.name: Idle User
campaigns.description: Fires when the user is idle for 10 seconds.

steps.title: idle_user
steps.intro:
Trigger: idle_user
You stopped all activity (mouse, keyboard, touch, scroll) for 10 seconds.

The timer pauses when the tab is hidden and resumes when you return.

campaigns.name: Time on Page
campaigns.description: Fires after 25 seconds on the page.

steps.title: time_on_page
steps.intro:
Trigger: time_on_page
You have been on this page for 25 seconds.

Unlike idle_user, this timer is not reset by activity.

campaigns.name: Exit Intent
campaigns.description: Fires when the mouse moves toward the top of the browser.

steps.title: exit_intent
steps.intro:
Trigger: exit_intent
Detected when the mouse left the document from the top edge (sensitivity: 15px).

Classic use case: show a discount before the user leaves.

steps.title: url_match
steps.intro:
Trigger: url_match
The current URL contains "campaigns".

matchType options: exact, contains, regex.
Fired after a 7 second delay.

steps.title: device_type — desktop
steps.intro:
Trigger: device_type
Detected device: desktop (width > 1024px, no mobile UA).

You can create separate campaigns for mobile, tablet, and desktop.

steps.title: device_type — tablet
steps.intro:
Trigger: device_type
Detected device: tablet (width 769–1024px).

Each device type runs its own campaign independently.

steps.title: device_type — mobile
steps.intro:
Trigger: device_type
Detected device: mobile (width ≤ 768px or mobile UA).

Useful for showing mobile-only onboarding.

campaigns.name: Returning User
campaigns.description: Fires when the user has visited before.

steps.title: returning_user
steps.intro:
Trigger: returning_user
Welcome back! You have visited this page before.

minVisits: 1 means at least 1 previous visit is required. Frequency: once — fires only the first time you return.

campaigns.name: Session Count
campaigns.description: Fires when the session count exceeds 1.

steps.title: session_count
steps.intro:
Trigger: session_count
You have opened this page more than 1 time.

operator options: equal, greater, less.
Each page load = one session.

steps.title: element_click
steps.intro:
Trigger: element_click
You clicked on #click-target.

Uses closest() internally, so clicking on any child element of the target also fires the trigger.

campaigns.name: Element Hover
campaigns.description: Fires when the user hovers over #hover-target for 800ms.

campaigns.name: Form Interaction
campaigns.description: Fires when the user focuses on a field inside #contact-form.

steps.title: form_interaction
steps.intro:
Trigger: form_interaction
You focused on a field inside #contact-form.

interactionType options: focus, input, change.

campaigns.name: Custom Event
campaigns.description: Fires when a 'demo_campaign_event' CustomEvent is dispatched.

steps.title: custom_event
steps.intro:
Trigger: custom_event
Fired by dispatching a DOM event named demo_campaign_event.

Use document.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('demo_campaign_event')) from anywhere in your app.

campaigns.name: Scroll Depth
campaigns.description: Fires when the user scrolls past 60% of the page.

steps.title: scroll_depth
steps.intro:
Trigger: scroll_depth
You scrolled past 60% of the page.

Fires once per session. Common use case: newsletter signup after user shows intent by reading.

steps.title: scroll_to_element
steps.intro:
Trigger: scroll_to_element
This element is now 50% visible in the viewport (threshold: 0.5).

Unlike element_visible, this does not fire on the initial check — only on scroll.

steps.title: element_visible
steps.intro:
Trigger: element_visible
This element reached 70% visibility (threshold: 0.7).

Checks on both initial page load and every scroll/resize event.
